_comment: Post-COVID vol crush — VIX drops from elevated window (~53 mean) to 38.15. Exercises downward-move axis: severity uses abs(z_score), so a vol crush scores the same as a spike of equal magnitude. The system cannot distinguish 'good' vol drops from 'bad' spikes.

expected_band.rationale_pending_trader: SRS CLS-001 ECDF (downward move axis): VIX median=52.225, |deviation|=|38.15-52.225|=14.075. Count of deviations ≤ 14.075: 18 out of 20 (only the spike residual 14.465 and extreme 19.775 exceed this), so ecdf_rank=18, severity=18/20=0.90. The vol crush is ranked high because deviations this large are rare in the elevated window, but ECDF cannot distinguish 'good' crushes (risk-off, profit) from 'bad' spikes (risk-on, pain). Both have |deviation| ≈ 14. Tanh gives 0.07 (|z|=1.38, underestimates). ECDF 0.90 correctly shows this is a large move within regime, but downstream logic (e.g., sign of move, signal category) must interpret direction.
